title.zh: 鄰居效應的真相：印度選舉數據揭開地方候選人拉票密碼
title.en: The Hidden Network: How Local Candidates Win Votes in India

summary.zh: 在印度的鄉野村鎮，一個看似普通的選舉現象背後，竟藏着改變選情的關鍵密碼——『鄰居效應』。多年來，學界觀察到地方候選人總能獲得過半數選票，但為何本地人更願意投給鄰居？這篇《政治地理學》的新研究揭開了謎底：候選人背後的社交網絡，才是拉動選票的隱形繩索。研究團隊透過精準匹配的微觀投票站數據，分析超過53萬個投票點，發現地方候選人不僅靠『熟人』優勢，更透過既有社交網絡擴展動員範圍，將選民觸角延伸至傳統選舉地圖之外。這就像一張無形的蜘蛛網，從家門口延伸到村口，讓選民在『熟人推薦』下自然傾向支持本地候選人。研究證明，『鄉土情結』背後，是精心設計的社會網絡戰術，而非單純的鄉土情懷。
summary.en: In the villages and towns of India, a seemingly ordinary election phenomenon holds a hidden key to victory: the 'friends and neighbours effect'. For decades, scholars have observed that local candidates consistently win the majority of votes, yet the reasons behind this pattern have remained murky. This groundbreaking study in *Political Geography* reveals that the real driver is not just familiarity but the strategic use of existing social networks. By analyzing micro-level polling station data across 534,552 voting locations, researchers discovered that candidates leverage their pre-existing community ties to amplify voter mobilization beyond traditional campaign efforts. Like an invisible web, these networks extend the campaign’s reach, turning casual acquaintances into active supporters. The findings challenge conventional wisdom, showing that local advantage stems less from innate 'hometown loyalty' and more from deliberate, resource-driven strategies that harness the power of community connections. This insight reshapes how we understand electoral mobilization in democracies worldwide.

keyArticle.title: How local candidates mobilize voters: Evidence from India
